message:
fix(vdbe): fix NULL value handling in WHERE clause comparisons (bd-36eh.1)

The WHERE clause filtering using Ne opcode was not correctly skipping
rows with NULL column values. When comparing col=value where col is
NULL, the Ne opcode returned should_jump=false (because of SQL three-
valued logic), causing NULL rows to incorrectly pass through the filter.

Fix: Set NULLEQ flag (0x80) in p5 for Ne opcodes in emit_where_filter.
This makes NULL != value return true, correctly skipping rows with
NULL values while preserving standard SQL semantics.

Changes:
- codegen.rs: Add NULLEQ flag (0x80) to Ne opcodes in emit_where_filter
- lib.rs: Enable three previously-ignored tests for NULL handling
- connection.rs: Fix &[] to None for trigger statement execution params

Tests enabled:
- index_with_null_values
- index_update_null_to_non_null
- index_update_non_null_to_null

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
